cards.back: Set the DC operating point (Q-point) in the active region so the BJT can amplify AC signals without distortion.
IB = (VCC − VBE)/RB. Problem: IC = βIB depends directly on β — unstable.
VTh = R2·VCC/(R1+R2). R2 is the bottom resistor connected to ground.
RTh = R1||R2 = R1·R2/(R1+R2). Found by shorting VCC and looking into the divider.
VTh = IB·RTh + VBE + IE·RE. Solve for IB using IE = (β+1)IB.
(β+1)RE >> RTh. When true, IE ≈ (VTh−VBE)/RE, independent of β.
Negative feedback: IC↑ → IE↑ → VE=IERE↑ → VBE↓ → IB↓ → IC↓. Self-correcting.
I_divider >> IB (typically ≥10×). Ensures VB isn't affected by IB variations.
RB connects collector to base. If IC↑ → VC↓ → IB↓ → IC↓. Moderate stability, simpler than voltage-divider.

cards.front: Which bias config used in all tutorial Qs?
cards.back: Voltage-divider bias with R1, R2, RC, RE. Industry standard. Excellent β-independence.
